questions.question: What is the defining property of a Binary Search Tree (BST)?
options: Every node has exactly two children
Left subtree keys are smaller and right subtree keys are larger than the node key
Nodes are stored in sorted array order
All leaves appear at the same level

questions.question: In a BST, what traversal outputs keys in sorted order?
BST search chooses left or right subtree based on:
options: Target key comparison with current node key

questions.question: When inserting into a BST, where is the new node placed?
options: At first internal node with one child
At the search-failure null position as a leaf

questions.question: What happens if you try to insert a duplicate key in the standard BST model from lecture?
options: Duplicate is added to left subtree
Duplicate is added to right subtree

questions.question: Deleting a BST node that is a leaf requires:
options: Detaching that node from its parent

questions.question: Deleting a BST node with one child is done by:
options: Connecting child directly to deleted node's parent

questions.question: For deleting a BST node with two children, a common method is to replace it with:
Why does deleting using the largest key in the left subtree work cleanly?
options: That node cannot have a left child
That node cannot have a right child
That node is always a leaf

options: The comparison field used for ordering

options: 40 is replaced by its right child
40 is simply removed; 30 has no right child

questions.question: BST: root 50, left 30 (with right child 40), right 70. Delete node 30 (one child). 30 is replaced by:
options: 40 connects directly to 50 as left child

questions.question: BST: root 50, left subtree has max key 45. You delete root 50 using the 'largest in left subtree' method. The replacement value is:
After replacing 50 with 45 in that deletion, what must happen to node 45 in the left subtree?
options: It must be deleted from its original position
It is moved to right subtree
The whole left subtree is rebuilt

questions.question: The BST inorder predecessor of node 50 (with left subtree) is:
options: The leftmost node in right subtree
The rightmost node in left subtree

questions.question: Node 45 used as inorder predecessor in deletion cannot have a right child because:
options: If it had a larger right child, that child would be the predecessor instead
BST does not allow right children in left subtrees
It is the minimum of the tree
